planets.name: Mercury
planets.description: Mercury is the smallest planet in our solar system and the one closest to the Sun. Its surface is heavily cratered and lacks a substantial atmosphere.

planets.name: Venus
planets.description: Venus has a dense atmosphere rich in carbon dioxide, creating a runaway greenhouse effect that makes it the hottest planet.

planets.name: Earth
planets.description: Earth is the only known planet to support life, with abundant liquid water and a balanced atmosphere of nitrogen and oxygen.

planets.name: Jupiter
planets.description: Jupiter is the largest planet and is known for its Great Red Spot, a persistent storm system larger than Earth.

dwarf_planets.name: Pluto
dwarf_planets.description: Pluto resides in the Kuiper Belt and has a thin atmosphere that freezes and falls as snow when it drifts farther from the Sun.
